Positive Indicator: Accurately retrieves details and past context (“I remember we decided that last quarter.”)
Negative Indicator: Frequently forgets recent decisions or details (“I can’t recall what we agreed on.”)

Positive Indicator: Maintains focus for extended discussions without prompting (“I’ve been following this thread the whole time.”)
Negative Indicator: Loses focus quickly; message thread drifts (“I zoned out halfway through your point.”)

Positive Indicator: Makes routine choices promptly (“Let’s proceed with option B now.”)
Negative Indicator: Hesitates or delays simple decisions (“I’m not sure which to pick; can I sleep on it?”)

Positive Indicator: Offers multiple solutions rapidly when issues arise (“We could A, B, or C to resolve that.”)
Negative Indicator: Struggles to propose or refine solutions; stalls when challenges emerge

Positive Indicator: Juggles several tasks or details in mind (“I’m tracking tasks X, Y, and Z.”)
Negative Indicator: Drops threads when context shifts; misses steps in multi-part tasks

Positive Indicator: Generates varied ideas quickly during brainstorming (“Here are five fresh angles we could explore.”)
Negative Indicator: Experiences mental blocks; only offers one idea or repeats the same thought

Positive Indicator: Smoothly transitions between topics or projects without losing context
Negative Indicator: Gets overwhelmed when switching tasks (“I’m all over the place trying to switch gears.”)

Positive Indicator: Interprets new information swiftly and integrates it (“I understood that update right away.”)
Negative Indicator: Reads the same message repeatedly to comprehend; slow to grasp new inputs

Positive Indicator: Sustains mental effort over long sessions (“I’m still sharp after this two-hour workshop.”)
Negative Indicator: Becomes mentally drained in short intervals; can’t maintain effort beyond brief periods

Positive Indicator: Balances new info with ongoing tasks without overload
Negative Indicator: Shows overwhelm when too many items are introduced; drops or neglects some tasks

Positive Indicator: Adapts thinking when plans change; pivots strategies fluidly (“Given the new data, let’s try a different model.”)
Negative Indicator: Resists adjusting approach when circumstances shift

Positive Indicator: Quickly spots inconsistencies or typos in posts (“I caught that typo before it went out.”)
Negative Indicator: Misses obvious mistakes or logical flaws in messages (“I didn’t even see that error until later.”)

Positive Indicator: Asks targeted questions only when genuinely needed (“Can you clarify which file you mean?”)
Negative Indicator: Repeatedly requests clarifications for simple points, indicating poor initial comprehension

Positive Indicator: References and follows up on earlier points (“As I noted two days ago, we still need to…” )
Negative Indicator: Fails to circle back on key items or action requests (“I never heard back about my question from yesterday.”)

Positive Indicator: Quickly applies new feedback or info (“Got it—updated the deck as you suggested.”)
Negative Indicator: Struggles to incorporate feedback; repeats mistakes (“I still did it the old way even after your suggestion.”)

Positive Indicator: Reports feeling energized and ready to tackle work (“I’ve got plenty of energy today.”)
Negative Indicator: Describes being wiped out or “running on empty” (“I’m completely wiped,” “I need a nap.”)

Positive Indicator: Mentions being physically comfortable or pain-free (“My back feels fine today.”)
Negative Indicator: Complains of headaches, muscle aches, tension (“My shoulders are killing me.”)

Positive Indicator: Refers to restful, sufficient sleep (“I slept like a log last night and feel great.”)
Negative Indicator: Notes insomnia, oversleeping, or unrefreshing sleep (“I still feel tired after eight hours.”)

Positive Indicator: Bounces back quickly after breaks or time off (“I feel refreshed after my weekend away.”)
Negative Indicator: Takes excessive time to recover energy after a break; still sluggish afterward

Positive Indicator: Takes regular short breaks and returns promptly (“Stepping away for a quick stretch—back in 5.”)
Negative Indicator: Frequently steps away for extended periods or skips returning (“Be right back… been gone a while.”)

Positive Indicator: Mentions staying hydrated or well-fed (“Just grabbed my coffee—feeling good.”)
Negative Indicator: Notes skipping meals or dehydration (“I haven’t eaten since morning and I’m dragging.”)

Positive Indicator: Talks about intentional self-care actions (“Scheduled a walk after this meeting.”)
Negative Indicator: Ignores self-care suggestions or reports none (“I haven’t had time to move today.”)

Positive Indicator: Mentions feeling mobile and limber (“Feeling agile after that stretch.”)
Negative Indicator: Expresses need to stretch or move due to stiffness (“I need to get up and walk around.”)

Positive Indicator: Notes that their workspace is supportive (“My desk setup is helping my posture.”)
Negative Indicator: Describes uncomfortable workspace causing fatigue (“My chair is dreadful—so tired of this posture.”)

Positive Indicator: Wraps up with upbeat sign-offs (“Great session—feel ready for tomorrow!”)
Negative Indicator: Signs off early or comments on feeling spent at day’s end (“That’s all I can do today—I’m beat.”)

Positive Indicator: Expresses eagerness or vitality (“I’m really looking forward to this next step!”)
Negative Indicator: Conveys feeling drained or flat (“I have no emotional energy left for this.”)

Positive Indicator: Maintains a consistent, balanced tone throughout discussions
Negative Indicator: Shows abrupt shifts in mood or frequent irritability (“I was fine, and now I’m suddenly furious.”)

Positive Indicator: Actively responds with supportive or affirmative language (“That makes sense, I’m on board.”)
Negative Indicator: Uses minimal or disengaged replies (“Okay,” “Sure,” with no further comment)

Positive Indicator: Discusses pressure calmly and constructively (“Tight deadline, but we’ll handle it methodically.”)
Negative Indicator: Reacts to stress with panic or defeatist remarks (“I can’t handle another urgent request.”)

Positive Indicator: Offers understanding or concern (“That sounds stressful—let me know how I can help.”)
Negative Indicator: Fails to acknowledge others’ feelings or offers no support when they express distress

Positive Indicator: Uses exclamation or upbeat phrasing (“This is awesome—can’t wait to dive in!”)
Negative Indicator: Uses flat or resigned language (“I guess we’ll do it, if we have to.”)

Positive Indicator: Opens up about feelings appropriately (“I’m feeling a bit overwhelmed today.”)
Negative Indicator: Avoids any mention of emotions or dismisses them (“Let’s stick to facts—no feelings.”)

Positive Indicator: Says “thank you” or acknowledges help (“Thanks for stepping up on that.”)
Negative Indicator: Omits any thanks or recognition when others assist

Positive Indicator: Asks for help or input when needed (“Can someone give me feedback on this?”)
Negative Indicator: Refuses offers of assistance or never asks for help even when struggling

Positive Indicator: Compliments teammates’ efforts (“Great work on that presentation!”)
Negative Indicator: Fails to recognize or comment on others’ contributions

Positive Indicator: Acknowledges emotions but reins them in (“I’m upset, but let’s keep going.”)
Negative Indicator: Lets emotions dominate communication (“I’m so angry I can’t even think straight.”)

Positive Indicator: Rarely uses negative descriptors; focuses on solutions
Negative Indicator: Uses complaint-heavy language or constant criticism (“Everything here is a disaster.”)

Positive Indicator: Shares frustrations rarely and briefly
Negative Indicator: Frequently vents frustrations without segue into problem-solving (“This is so frustrating, ugh!”)

Positive Indicator: Frames challenges constructively (“This is tough, but it’s an opportunity to learn.”)
Negative Indicator: Frames situations pessimistically (“This will never work; it’s pointless to try.”)

Positive Indicator: Uses inclusive language (“Let’s tackle this together as a team.”)
Negative Indicator: Communicates with cold detachment or isolation (“Do it yourself—I have no time for this.”)
